title: uragoga pachyphylla
intro: Uragoga pachyphylla is a plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It has been traditionally used in African and Asian cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Uragoga pachyphylla include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a diuretic and to improve blood circulation. Uragoga pachyphylla is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use, with the roots and leaves being the primary sources of the plant's medicinal compounds.
